content:
- Processed PO mail request to split tests into make test + make test-long, migrate references, create ultra-high-priority tasks, and continue ralph.
- Sent frequent PO email updates at each phase (ack, plan, start edits, blocker, continue-ralph start, completion).
- Updated Makefile to only keep test and test-long targets. test skips exactly the three ultra-long stress e2e tests; test-long runs only those and prints explicit warning to move shortened tests back.
- Ran broad repo migration (excluding .ralph/progress and .ralph/archive) replacing legacy test target mentions. Normalized resulting task/skill wording, including skill templates.
- Ensured test-long requirement remains only in deep/meta scope: recurring meta task 18, deep verification task 33, plus related meta pass context entries. Added explicit policy that if test-long fails, create shorter real-binary e2e regression reproducer.
- Created two new ultra-high-priority tasks:
  - 40-task-ultra-high-prio-test-target-split-and-reference-migration.md
  - 41-task-ultra-high-prio-split-ultra-long-e2e-into-short-parallel-tests.md
- Ran continue-ralph skill sequence. Initial task_switch failed because .ralph/current_task.txt was missing. Recovered by restoring pointer file to task 40 and rerunning task_switch.
- Continued ralph successfully: service now active after timeout-detach flow.
- Committed changes as: 3c8fa7d "auto-commit before ralph continue".

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` to pick the next task (Choose-Task agent).
- Verified all existing bug tasks show `<passes>true</passes>` / `<passing>true</passing>`; no broken bug tasks found.
- Found multiple non-bug tasks with `<passes>false</passes>`, including two tagged `<priority>ultra-high</priority>` in `story-rust-system-harness`.
- Chose the first ultra-high task in that story chain: `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/40-task-ultra-high-prio-test-target-split-and-reference-migration.md` and wrote it to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Next: switch to Do-Task agent to execute task 40 and revalidate `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`.

- Read previous progress log entry.
- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and opened task `40-task-ultra-high-prio-test-target-split-and-reference-migration.md`.
- Next: use parallel explorer agents to scan Makefile/test targets and find legacy target references across repo/tasks/skills.

- New PO mail asked: "Which tests are too long? Do the new tasks say?"
- Treated as research/investigation only (no code changes).
- Read prior progress, then inspected Makefile and new tasks 40/41.
- Confirmed exact ultra-long tests are explicitly listed in Makefile test selectors:
  1) ha::e2e_multi_node::e2e_multi_node_stress_planned_switchover_concurrent_sql
  2) ha::e2e_multi_node::e2e_multi_node_stress_unassisted_failover_concurrent_sql
  3) ha::e2e_multi_node::e2e_multi_node_stress_no_quorum_fencing_with_concurrent_sql
- Confirmed tasks 40/41 do NOT name these test IDs explicitly; they refer to "agreed/current ultra-long tests" generically.
- Sent 5 PO email updates as required: acknowledge, plan, subtask start, subtask complete/move, final summary answer.
- No blockers.
- Next if asked: can create a task to make task docs explicitly enumerate the three long test IDs for tighter traceability.

- Subagent scan complete for legacy test target references across `.agents/skills/**` and repo (excluding `.ralph/progress/**` and `.ralph/archive/**`).
- Found no active legacy make test target names; only `make test` and `make test-long` are invoked as make targets.
- Main cleanup need is contradictory/duplicated gate wording: many files still list `make test` twice with stale “BDD/full regular” phrasing and little/no `make test-long` usage.
- Noted specific hotspots for normalization: `.agents/skills/*`, `.ralph/ralph-do-task.md`, `RUST_SYSTEM_HARNESS_PLAN.md`, and many `.ralph/tasks/**` acceptance blocks.

- New PO mail asked for evidence-based timing and explicitly requested no test execution.
- Followed investigation-only flow: scanned `.ralph/evidence` and extracted timing data from `.ralph/evidence/27-e2e-ha-stress/*.summary.json`.
- Verified exact mapping from Rust test names to evidence scenario names via `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` scenario_name constants.
- Aggregated per-test timing stats (latest, min/avg/max across runs) and observed no-quorum fencing is much longer (passed runs ~126s to ~297s; avg ~191s) while planned switchover ~21s and unassisted failover ~25s.
- Noted one failed no-quorum summary entry with 0 ms duration (early failure artifact).
- Sent frequent PO updates (5 emails): ack/start, plan/path discovery, subtask start, subtask complete, final summary.
- No code/test changes; no blockers.
- Next if asked: can extract same timing report into a persistent evidence markdown/table under `.ralph/evidence`.

- New PO follow-up asked whether any other tests are long and requested task alteration: only test #3 in make test-long if it is the sole 3min+ case.
- Followed email cadence strictly: sent ack/start, plan, timing completion, consistency completion, and final summary.
- Recomputed durations from `.ralph/evidence/27-e2e-ha-stress/*.summary.json` using `(finished_at_unix_ms - started_at_unix_ms)`.
- Confirmed only `ha::e2e_multi_node::e2e_multi_node_stress_no_quorum_fencing_with_concurrent_sql` is >=3min (passed runs 126357..297266 ms). Other stress tests remain ~21s and ~24-25s.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/40-task-ultra-high-prio-test-target-split-and-reference-migration.md` to codify the 3-minute policy and explicitly map current tests (`test-long` only no-quorum; other two in `make test`).
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/41-task-ultra-high-prio-split-ultra-long-e2e-into-short-parallel-tests.md` to reflect current single 3min+ scenario and clarify the other two stress tests are regular `make test` scope.
- No codebase runtime/test fixes performed; only requested `.ralph` task updates and evidence-backed analysis.
- Next if asked: apply the same policy to Makefile selectors directly (currently still lists all 3 in test-long), then run gates.

- Spawned parallel explorer agents to scan Makefile/test targets and legacy gate references.
- Findings: Makefile already only defines `test`/`test-long`; repo has no `#[ignore]` tests; most drift is duplicated/contradictory wording (many tasks say `make test` twice / “all BDD features pass”, templates/skills repeat `make test`, and `.ralph/ralph-do-task.md` repeats `make test`).
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/40-task-ultra-high-prio-test-target-split-and-reference-migration.md` and set marker to `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Next: perform deep skeptical verification pass, alter at least one concrete plan item, then promote marker to `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Received PO follow-up demanding explicit policy on the testlong split task: normal make test must hard-timeout at 2 minutes, run fully parallel, and never require serial (parallel fail/serial requirement are bugs); make test-long must have no timeout.
- Followed the mailbox update cadence and sent frequent status emails (ack, plan, start, complete, final pending).
- Updated only `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/41-task-ultra-high-prio-split-ultra-long-e2e-into-short-parallel-tests.md` as requested.
- Added the new policy statements in both Scope and acceptance criteria so enforcement intent is explicit and testable.
- No codebase/runtime fixes performed, no tests run, no other files changed by intent.
- Next if asked: mirror the same explicit policy language into related task docs (e.g. task 40) or Makefile enforcement implementation task details.
